.github/workflows/build.yml.general_idea: Update the GitHub Actions workflow to use Java 21 instead of Java 17 for the build environment.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent version from 2.6.7 to 3.3.13 to align with Java 21 and Jakarta EE namespace changes.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline and uses javax.* namespaces, causing runtime failures and incompatibility with modern libraries on JDK 21.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK baseline.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project must compile and run against JDK 21 features and bytecode version.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated MySQL connector artifact with the new group ID and artifact ID.
pom.xml.why_exists: The old artifact 'mysql:mysql-connector-java' is deprecated and may not be compatible with newer Spring Boot 3 drivers or JDBC specifications; the new artifact is the standard for Spring Boot 3.

pom.xml.general_idea: Remove standalone swagger-annotations dependency as it is now managed by SpringDoc.
Replace springdoc-openapi-ui with springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui for Spring Boot 3 compatibility.
pom.xml.why_exists: The old springdoc-openapi-ui artifact relies on javax.servlet and is incompatible with Spring Boot 3's Jakarta EE 9+ namespace.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add jakarta.validation-api dependency to replace javax.validation.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3 uses Jakarta EE namespaces; javax.validation classes are no longer available on the classpath by default in the same way, requiring explicit Jakarta dependencies if not provided by starter.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add flyway-mysql dependency to ensure MySQL support for Flyway in Spring Boot 3.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3 auto-configuration for Flyway may require explicit database-specific dependencies to function correctly with the new driver structure.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add jakarta.mail-api dependency for test scope.
pom.xml.why_exists: javax.mail is replaced by jakarta.mail in Spring Boot 3; tests relying on mail APIs need the Jakarta version.

pom.xml.general_idea: Remove explicit version from spotbugs-annotations to rely on Spring Boot BOM management.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Migrate validation annotation import from javax to jakarta namespace.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: javax.validation package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+ (Spring Boot 3.x), causing a compile error 'package javax.validation does not exist'.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Migrate validation constraint import from javax to jakarta namespace.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: javax.validation package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+ (Spring Boot 3.x); code fails to compile with 'package javax.validation.constraints does not exist'.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: The javax.validation package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+ and Spring Boot 3; using it causes a compile error (package not found).

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Migrate JPA annotations from javax.persistence to jakarta.persistence to align with Jakarta EE 9+ standards required by Spring Boot 3.x and Java 21.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: javax.persistence package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+; code fails to compile with 'package javax.persistence does not exist' errors.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with stricter serialization checks in Java 21.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: Java 21 enables stricter serialization checks by default; missing @Serial annotation on serialVersionUID triggers a compile error or warning treated as error.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with stricter serialization checks in Java 14+.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: While not strictly a compile error in all setups, modern Java tooling and linters flag missing @Serial on serialVersionUID as a violation of serialization best practices introduced in Java 14.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: The method getById() was removed in Spring Data JPA 3.0 (required by Spring Boot 3.x), causing a compilation error.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 17+ serialization best practices enforced by stricter compiler checks in Java 21.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: Java 21 (and recent Java 17/21 toolchains) may enforce stricter checks or warnings regarding serialization fields, requiring the @Serial annotation to explicitly mark serialVersionUID as a valid serialization field, preventing potential compile-time errors or warnings treated as failures in strict builds.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: Java 14+ (and stricter compilers/linters in Java 21) may flag or error on serialVersionUID fields lacking the @Serial annotation, as it is a synthetic field managed by the serialization mechanism.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is not available without importing java.io.Serial, causing a compilation error.

src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/enkarin/bookcros.why_exists: Compilation error: serialVersionUID must be annotated with @Serial in Java 14+ unless the class is a direct subclass of java.io.Serializable without other serializable fields, or to suppress warnings/errors in strict builds.
